title: Spironolactone
buzzwords: Weak collecting-duct diuretic
Competitive mineralocorticoid receptor antagonist
First-line for cirrhotic ascites
Mortality benefit in HFrEF NYHA II-IV
Treats hirsutism and PCOS via antiandrogen effect
Causes gynecomastia and decreased libido in men
Discontinue in pregnancy due to fetal harm
Aldactone, the prototype steroid-like aldosterone blocker

title: Eplerenone
buzzwords: Selective collecting-duct aldosterone blocker
Mineralocorticoid receptor antagonist with fewer sex-steroid effects
Used in HFrEF and resistant hypertension
Minimal antiandrogen activity
No gynecomastia compared with its sibling agent
Causes hyperkalemia and type IV RTA
Selective MR antagonist avoiding sex-hormone side effects
Inspra

title: Amiloride
buzzwords: Weak distal-nephron diuretic
Does not act through aldosterone receptor
Often combined with hydrochlorothiazide to prevent hypokalemia
No antiandrogen or gynecomastia side effects
Blocks apical sodium channel in principal cells
Treats lithium-induced nephrogenic diabetes insipidus
Direct ENaC blocker, K+-sparing without MR antagonism
Midamor

title: Triamterene
buzzwords: Mild K+-sparing diuretic, not an MR antagonist
Blocks luminal sodium channel in collecting duct
Packaged with hydrochlorothiazide as Dyazide/Maxzide
Counters thiazide-induced hypokalemia
No gynecomastia, unlike steroid-based siblings
Can cause hyperkalemia and type IV RTA
Pteridine ENaC blocker (non-amiloride)
Dyrenium
